scientific_integrity.detail: No fabricated statistics, p-values, or citations were produced across all evaluated outputs; all claims trace to input data or documented tool behavior.

practice_boundaries.detail: All outputs remained within chemistry and laboratory computation scope; no unsupported clinical or diagnostic recommendations were made.

methodological_ground.detail: No methodological fallacies detected; outputs apply standard approaches appropriate to chemistry and laboratory computation tasks.

code_usability.detail: Generated code executed without syntax errors or unresolvable dependency failures across all tested inputs.

functional_suitability.note: Risk score aggregation formula now documented; pre-flight scope gate added; all v1 gaps closed

reliability.note: Fallback template with SMILES parse error guidance; stress-case checklist present; pre-flight gate prevents partial ADMET output

performance_context.note: SKILL.md is 181 lines — well-sized; no references/ folder but content is self-contained

agent_usability.note: Pre-flight scope gate in Workflow Step 1; false positive/negative caveat now mandatory in Output Requirements; risk score aggregation documented

human_usability.note: Description is precise and discoverable for medicinal chemists; SMILES requirement clearly stated

security.note: No credentials; RDKit local execution; no external API calls; SMILES input is structured data with low injection risk

maintainability.note: Alert patterns in SKILL.md table; references to published literature; clean script separation; all fixes applied cleanly

agent_specific.note: Pre-flight gate closes partial ADMET output gap; escape hatches strong; composability still limited by single-molecule input model

key_strengths: Risk score aggregation formula (weighted sum: HIGH=1.0, MEDIUM=0.5, LOW=0.2, normalized to [0,1]) now fully documented, enabling reproducible multi-alert scoring
Pre-flight scope gate in Workflow Step 1 prevents any partial ADMET output before refusal, closing the primary v1 boundary gap
False positive/negative caveat now mandatory in Output Requirements, ensuring consistent transparency across all outputs
Comprehensive toxicophore library with 10 alert structures, risk levels, and published literature references
Audit-ready commands with concrete SMILES examples enable rapid functional verification

recommendations.title: Refusal message wording may deviate from template in edge cases
recommendations.problem: The scope refusal for ADMET requests occasionally shows minor wording variation from the documented template.
recommendations.root_cause: Input Validation section does not mark the refusal message as verbatim-required.
recommendations.fix: Add 'respond verbatim:' before the refusal message template in Input Validation to enforce exact wording.

recommendations.title: No batch SMILES processing mode
recommendations.problem: The skill processes one SMILES string at a time, requiring repeated invocations for multi-compound screening workflows.
recommendations.root_cause: CLI design is single-molecule focused; no --batch or --file parameter documented.
recommendations.fix: Consider adding a --file parameter accepting a text file of SMILES strings (one per line) for batch screening, with per-molecule JSON output.
